regimeNotes: NEUTRAL regime maintained 2026-06-23. DXY at highest levels in over a year (estimated ~101-102), providing strong headwind for risk assets. 10Y Treasury yield ~4.48%, restrictive. Fed holds 3.50-3.75% but hawkish: 9 of 18 FOMC members project at least one hike in 2026; 6 of those 9 project two or more hikes — market pricing shifted from cuts to potential hikes. Crypto under broad pressure: BTC $62,424 (-2.74% 24h), ETH $1,654.76 (-5.35%), SOL $68.98 (-6.55%). Total crypto mcap $2.225T (-2.80% 24h). BTC dominance 56.22% stable. F&G index 23 (Extreme Fear, slightly up from 20). Pre-market stocks weak: SPY $735 (-1.26%), NVDA $202.30 (-3.04%), GOOGL $342.80 (-1.97%), GLD $378.85 (-1.49%). S&P 500 prior close ~$7,431 (near ATH) prevents full risk-off classification. EUR/USD 1.1420. Iran conflict complicating inflation/energy outlook. Dalio quadrant: stagflation-leaning (rates high, energy-driven inflation risk, growth slowing). Zero trades today — all positions above invalidation levels; crypto sleeve at 19.50% within 20% neutral cap; hold day is correct per no-overtrading mandate.

SPY.thesis: Core US equity beta ($744.39 close June 22, 13.29% NAV). S&P near ATH (~7,431) with resilient broad index despite hawkish Fed pivot. Diversified anchor; holds value even if specific names underperform.
SPY.invalidationLevel: Sustained close below 660 USD or confirmed Fed re-pricing toward multiple hikes pushing SPY into a technical downtrend.

NVDA.thesis: AI/compute secular leader at $208.65 close (above $208.44 avgCost, 14.79% NAV). AI capex cycle intact; NVDA raised $25B in bonds June 15 signaling management confidence. Highest-conviction growth driver for the 33% CAGR target.
NVDA.invalidationLevel: AI capex digestion or data-center demand rollover; close below ~150 USD.

MSFT.thesis: Quality AI/cloud compounder at $367.34 (below $391.98 avgCost, 14.25% NAV). Azure + Copilot revenue growing; temporary macro/rate headwind. Thesis intact — holding through weakness.
MSFT.invalidationLevel: Cloud growth deceleration or AI capex without demonstrable ROI; close below 340 USD.

GOOGL.thesis: Quality growth (search + cloud + AI) at $349.68 (below $366.53 avgCost, 14.00% NAV). Noam Shazeer departure flagged as AI talent risk but search monopoly and cloud revenue dominant; $84.75B capital raise funds $180-190B capex plan. HOLD.
GOOGL.invalidationLevel: Search share erosion to AI rivals causing revenue decline; close below 310 USD.

UNH.thesis: Defensive value at $406.68 (+1.43% June 22, below $408.52 avgCost, 6.09% NAV). Medical-cost and regulatory headwinds; retained as defensive ballast and portfolio diversifier. Performing well on risk-off days.
UNH.invalidationLevel: Further regulatory or medical-cost shock; close below 360 USD.

GLD.thesis: Tail/debasement hedge at $384.59 (below $386.54 avgCost, 4.33% NAV). Central-bank buying supportive; strong DXY is a headwind but small size (4.33%) prevents meaningful drag on 33% CAGR target. Watching if DXY strength forces a further trim.
GLD.invalidationLevel: Real yields spike with strong USD reversal sustained; close below 350 USD.

BTC.thesis: Core crypto sleeve leader at $62,424 (below $64,629 avgCost, 14.27% NAV). F&G 23 (Extreme Fear) is contrarian bullish signal; BTC dominance 56.22% stable; IBIT ($36.50) confirms ETF channel still active. Well-positioned for next risk-on expansion when sleeve cap rises to 35%.

ETH.thesis: Discounted L1 leader at $1,654.76 (below $1,667.47 avgCost, 4.04% NAV). ETH now underwater — temporary in volatile market; asymmetric upside when risk-on returns and capital rotates into alts. Small position appropriate.
ETH.invalidationLevel: Structural loss of share to L1 rivals; close below 1,300 USD.

SOL.thesis: High-beta satellite at $68.98 (above $67.81 avgCost, 1.18% NAV). Trimmed to 16.842 shares to maintain neutral 20% crypto cap. Small enough to ride volatility; first to scale up when regime flips to risk-on and sleeve cap expands to 35%.
SOL.invalidationLevel: Break below 52 USD; network deterioration or sentiment collapse.
